dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker

Class JkDocker

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker.JkDocker

  • public class JkDocker
extends java.lang.Object
    Class providing utility methods for executing Docker commands.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static void assertPresent()
      Asserts that Docker is present and responsive on the system.
      static JkProcResult exec(java.lang.String dockerCommand, java.lang.String... params)
      Executes the specified Docker command with the given parameters and returns the result.
      static JkProcResult execCmdLine(java.lang.String dockerCommand, java.lang.String cmdLineArgs)
      Executes the specified Docker command with the given parameters expressed with a space separated string of arguments.
      static JkProcResult execJeka(java.lang.String... jekaCommandArgs)
      Executes Jeka in a docker container.

      Prepares a JkProcess object to execute Jeka in a Docker container.
      static boolean isPresent()
      Checks if Docker is present on the system.

      Prepares a JkProcess object to execute a Docker command with the given parameters.

      Prepares a JkProcess object to execute Jeka in a Docker container.

    • Constructor Detail

      • JkDocker

        public JkDocker()

    • Method Detail

      • exec

        public static JkProcResult exec(java.lang.String dockerCommand,
                                java.lang.String... params)
        Executes the specified Docker command with the given parameters and returns the result.
        Parameters:
        dockerCommand - The Docker command to execute as 'run', 'tag', 'version', ....
        params - The parameters to pass to the Docker command.
        Returns:
        The result of the command execution.

      • execCmdLine

        public static JkProcResult execCmdLine(java.lang.String dockerCommand,
                                       java.lang.String cmdLineArgs)
        Executes the specified Docker command with the given parameters expressed with a space separated string of arguments.
        Parameters:
        dockerCommand - The space separated String representing the arguments.
        cmdLineArgs - The parameters to pass to the Docker command as a space separated string (e.g. "-X -e run").
        See Also:
        exec(String, String...), (String)

      • prepareExec

        public static JkProcess prepareExec(java.lang.String dockerCommand,
                                    java.lang.String... params)
        Prepares a JkProcess object to execute a Docker command with the given parameters.

      • isPresent

        public static boolean isPresent()
        Checks if Docker is present on the system.

      • assertPresent

        public static void assertPresent()
        Asserts that Docker is present and responsive on the system.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the Docker daemon is not running or unresponsive.

      • getImageNames

        public static java.util.Set<java.lang.String> getImageNames()
        Prepares a JkProcess object to execute Jeka in a Docker container.

      • prepareExecJeka

        public static JkProcess prepareExecJeka(java.nio.file.Path workingDir,
                                        java.lang.String... jekaCommandArgs)
        Prepares a JkProcess object to execute Jeka in a Docker container. This can be useful for creating native executable runnable in a container.

        The latest jekadev/jeka image will be used to execute jeka.
        The container will be map volumes to execute Jeka in the working directory, and to fetch cache in $HOME/.jeka/cache4c:/cache dir.
        You can force a specific version of Jeka by adding -Djeka.version=xxx to jekaCommandArgs

        Parameters:
        jekaCommandArgs - The argument to pass to JeKa

      • prepareExecJeka

        public static JkProcess prepareExecJeka(java.lang.String... jekaCommandArgs)

      • execJeka

        public static JkProcResult execJeka(java.lang.String... jekaCommandArgs)
        Executes Jeka in a docker container.
        See Also:
        prepareExecJeka(Path, String...)
